title: Reflect Points Across Axes
why: Reflecting a point is the idea of opposites on a grid: same distance, the other direction. Warm up reading ordered pairs and flipping a direction (left↔right, up↔down) and reflections feel like a mirror.

diagnostic.q: What is the opposite direction of left?
A point is 3 steps right of 0. Its mirror is the same distance the other way. How far left?
In (x, y), which number tells how far up or down?

learn.heading: A mirror keeps the distance
learn.intro: A reflection is a mirror flip. The point stays the same distance from the line, but on the opposite side. So you flip one direction: left becomes right, or up becomes down — the distance does not change.
examples: Flip across the up/down line: a point 4 right becomes 4 left — same distance, other side.
Flip across the across line: a point 2 up becomes 2 down.

items.q: A. What is the opposite of up?
B. A point is 5 right of 0. Its mirror is the same distance left. How far left is it?
C. A point is 2 up. Its mirror is the same distance down. How far down?

tiers.intro: Keep the distance, flip the side.

items.q: A. A point is 7 steps right. Flipped to the other side, it is 7 steps ___.
items.hint: Flip right to its opposite: left. Distance stays 7.

items.q: B. A point is 6 steps up. Its mirror across the across-line is how many steps down?
A. Point (3, 2) flips left/right to the other side. Its distance from the center stays the same: ___ steps across.
items.hint: The x distance is 3, and a flip keeps the distance.

items.q: B. A point is 8 up. After flipping up/down, how far down is it?

exit.q: 1. A point is 4 steps up. Flipped to the other side, it is 4 steps ___.
exit.hint: Up flips to down; distance stays 4.

exit.q: 2. A point is 5 steps right. Its mirror is how many steps left?
